subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] nfsd/sunrpc: convert nfsstat server-side interfaces to use netlink

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the usefulness of adding per-procedure stats without userland consuming them, and asked for clarification on whether any existing code accesses this field.
reviews.raw_body:
This omission was intentional. The stated purpose of this patchset was
to convert nfsstat to use netlink. nfsstat does not access or display
this field today. Without something that consumes this stat, I don't
see the point of adding it here. We can always add it later when/if it
becomes useful to userland.

Is there something that accesses this today that I'm not aware of? If
not, is there some future intent to add this to nfsstat?
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the new netlink API should match the existing procfs API to prevent regressions and suggested mentioning the exclusion of a specific feature in the commit message.

Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that the 'wdeleg-getattr' field was missing from nfsstat's display, requesting its inclusion.
reviews.raw_body:
If we were adding a greenfield kernel-userspace API, I would agree.
However, this API is meant to be compatible with the existing
procfs API, so it should match to prevent a regression (I'm not
aware of a consumer, this is only a general guideline).

For future reference: since a missing feature cannot be expressed
in a patch's diff body, your intention to exclude this field could
be mentioned in the commit message.

---

I think nfsstat should display the wdeleg-getattr field. It has simply
been forgotten.


-- 
Chuck Lever

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Chuck Lever's concern about the lack of a netlink attribute to display the total number of operations, agreeing to add it in v5 and noting that an nfs-utils follow-on patch will be needed.
reviews.raw_body:
Ok. I'll plan to add it to the interface and send it in v5. nfs-utils
will need a follow-on patch to display the value. No promises on when
I'll do that, but it shouldn't be too hard.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author is addressing a concern about the patch's handling of server-side callback call counts, specifically wdeleg_getattr. They are proposing an alternative approach to expose these counts in nfsstat, rather than adding them as separate statistics.
reviews.raw_body:
I have a drafted patch, but I'm not thrilled with it. wdeleg_getattr is
weird in that it's not a statistic about server-side protocol
operations. So, it ends up sort of sitting off on its own in nfsstat.

What would you (and Dai) think about instead keeping and exposing
server-side callback call counts? Then we could just add a new section
to nfsstat server-side output with all of the callback operation counts
in it. wdeleg_getattr would just be the count for CB_GETATTRs.

Thoughts?
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-06-17.patch_summary: This patch series converts the nfsstat server-side interfaces to use netlink instead of /proc/net/rpc/nfsd. The new interface provides per-network-namespace procedure call counts and exposes them through a new netlink handler in the nfsd generic netlink family. This allows nfsstat to retrieve server statistics via netlink with a procfs fallback for older kernels. The patches add per-netns counters, switch svc_seq_show() to read from these counters, implement the server-stats-get netlink dump handler, and remove unused global arrays.
